w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Video provides a powerful way to help you prove your point.
When you click Online Video, you can paste in the embed code for the video you want to add.
You can also type a keyword to search online for the video that best fits your document.
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetuer adipiscing elit.
This document contains confidential business information and is intended solely for the use of the named parties.

WHEREAS, Licensor is the owner of certain proprietary software, systems, platforms, and related intellectual property, including but not limited to source code, object code, algorithms, technical documentation, and associated know-how, collectively identified as the

Licensor desires to grant to Licensee, and Licensee desires to obtain from Licensor, a limited, non-exclusive license to use, access, deploy, and integrate the Licensed Software and related Technology
(as defined herein) in connection with Licensee's business operations, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement;
the Parties desire to establish the terms and conditions governing the provision of professional services, technical support, maintenance, and potential technology transfer between them;
the Parties acknowledge that this Agreement represents a significant commercial relationship requiring careful and comprehensive legal documentation to protect the interests of both Parties;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants, representations, warranties, and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

As used in this Agreement and all Exhibits, Schedules, and addenda attached hereto, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below. Capitalized terms not defined in this Article shall have the meanings ascribed to them elsewhere in this Agreement.

Any entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a Party, where "control" means ownership of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting equity interests of such entity.

This Master Services and License Agreement, together with all Exhibits, Schedules, Addenda, and amendments hereto, as may be modified or supplemented from time to time in accordance with Section 18.4.

Those employees, contractors, agents, and representatives of Licensee and its Affiliates who are authorized by Licensee to access and use the Licensed Software solely in accordance with the terms hereof and whose names or roles are set forth on Schedule A.

All Intellectual Property owned or controlled by either Party prior to the Effective Date or independently developed by a Party outside the scope of this Agreement without use of the other Party's Confidential Information.
A written instrument executed by both Parties setting forth agreed-upon changes to the scope of Services, fees, timelines, or other material terms of a Statement of Work.
information, data, know-how, trade secrets, technical specifications, business plans, financial projections, customer data, or

other information disclosed by one Party to the other that is marked as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.
All work product, reports, documentation, software, modifications, configurations, and other tangible or intangible items produced by Licensor for Licensee pursuant to a Statement of Work.
All user manuals, technical specifications, API references, training materials, release notes, and other written materials provided by Licensor in connection with the Licensed Software.
March 1, 2026, or such other date as may be mutually agreed in writing by the authorized representatives of both Parties.
All amounts payable by Licensee to Licensor under this Agreement, including License Fees, Service Fees, Maintenance Fees, and any other charges as specified in Schedule B.

Any event beyond the reasonable control of a Party, including acts of God, natural disasters, pandemics, epidemics, war, terrorism, civil unrest, labor disputes, governmental actions, power outages, or failures of telecommunications infrastructure.

All patents, patent applications, copyrights, trademarks, service marks, trade names, trade secrets, know-how, moral rights, database rights, and all other intellectual property and proprietary rights, whether registered or unregistered, in any jurisdiction.
Platform software suite, including all modules identified in Exhibit A, together with all Updates and Upgrades provided under this Agreement.

Licensor's proprietary enterprise resource planning, analytics, and integration platform, comprised of the modules, APIs, and subsystems described in Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference.
Any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person processed in connection with this Agreement, as further defined in Exhibit D (Data Processing Addendum).
Implementation, configuration, customization, training, consulting, and other professional services provided by Licensor to Licensee as specified in one or more Statements of Work.
The service level agreement set forth in Exhibit C, establishing the performance standards, uptime commitments, and remedies applicable to the Hosted Services.
A written document executed by both Parties describing the scope, deliverables, timelines, fees, and other terms applicable to a specific set of Professional Services.
The period commencing on the Effective Date and continuing for three (3) years, unless earlier terminated or extended in accordance with Article XIV.
Software, libraries, or other components incorporated into the Licensed Software that are owned by third parties and subject to open-source or other third-party license terms, as listed in Exhibit E.
Bug fixes, patches, and minor enhancements to the Licensed Software that Licensor generally makes available to its licensees at no additional charge.
New versions or major releases of the Licensed Software that contain significant new functionality, which Licensor may provide pursuant to a separate written agreement.
ARTICLE II — LICENSE GRANT AND RESTRICTIONS

Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, including the timely payment of all applicable Fees, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable license during the Term to: (a) install and use the Licensed Software solely for Licensee's internal business purposes; (b) permit Authorized Users to access and use the Licensed Software in accordance with the Documentation; (c) make a reasonable number of copies of the Documentation for internal use only; and (d) integrate the Licensed Software with Licensee's existing systems using the APIs provided in the Licensed Software, subject to the API usage limits set forth in Schedule B.

Licensee shall not, and shall ensure that its Authorized Users do not, directly or indirectly: (a) sublicense, sell, resell, transfer, assign, or otherwise dispose of the Licensed Software or any rights therein to any third party; (b) copy, modify, translate, adapt, or create derivative works based upon the Licensed Software, except as expressly permitted herein; (c) reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile, or otherwise attempt to derive the source code, object code, or underlying structure of the Licensed Software; (d) remove or alter any proprietary notices, labels, or marks on the Licensed Software or Documentation; (e) use the Licensed Software in any manner that violates applicable law; (f) benchmark the Licensed Software and publish such results without Licensor's prior written consent; (g) access or use the Licensed Software to build a competing product or service; or (h) permit any third party to access or use the Licensed Software except as expressly authorized herein.

Where Licensee has subscribed to Hosted Services as indicated in Schedule B, Licensor shall make the Licensed Software available to Licensee on a software-as-a-service basis, subject to the SLA set forth in Exhibit C. In such case, the provisions of this Section 2 relating to installation shall not apply, and Licensee's access to the Licensed Software shall be governed by the applicable SLA and the terms of this Agreement.

Licensor reserves all rights not expressly granted in this Agreement. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to grant Licensee any rights in the Licensed Software or any other Licensor property, except as expressly provided herein. Licensee acknowledges that it acquires only a limited license to use the Licensed Software and does not acquire any ownership interest therein.

The Licensed Software may contain Third-Party Components subject to open-source license terms. Licensor shall provide Licensee with a list of all Third-Party Components as set forth in Exhibit E, together with the applicable license terms. To the extent any open-source license terms conflict with this Agreement, the open-source license terms shall govern with respect to those specific components.
Licensor shall perform the Professional Services described in each Statement of Work executed by the Parties. Each Statement of Work shall be governed by this

and, in the event of any conflict, this Agreement shall control unless the Statement of Work expressly states that a specific provision of the Statement of Work shall supersede a specified provision of this Agreement.

Each Statement of Work shall set forth: (a) a detailed description of the Services to be performed; (b) the Deliverables to be produced; (c) the timeline and milestones; (d) the applicable fees and payment schedule; (e) the personnel to be assigned; (f) any assumptions, dependencies, or prerequisites; and (g) any acceptance criteria applicable to Deliverables. No Statement of Work shall be binding unless executed in writing by authorized representatives of both Parties.

Either Party may request changes to the scope of Services in a pending or active Statement of Work by submitting a written change request to the other Party. Licensor shall evaluate such request and provide a written Change Order proposal within ten (10) business days. No change to any Statement of Work shall be effective unless memorialized in a Change Order executed by authorized representatives of both Parties.

Each Party shall designate a project manager who shall serve as the primary point of contact for all matters relating to the Professional Services. Project managers shall meet at least bi-weekly during active Service engagements to review progress, address issues, and coordinate activities. Either Party may replace its project manager upon written notice to the other.

Upon completion of each Deliverable, Licensor shall provide Licensee with written notice and the Deliverable for review. Licensee shall review each Deliverable within fifteen (15) business days after receipt and either: (a) provide written acceptance; or (b) provide written notice of rejection specifying, in reasonable detail, the

the Deliverable fails to conform to the applicable acceptance criteria. If Licensee does not respond within the review period, the Deliverable shall be deemed accepted. Upon rejection, Licensor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to correct the identified deficiencies and resubmit the Deliverable.

Licensee shall: (a) provide Licensor with timely access to Licensee's systems, facilities, personnel, and information reasonably necessary for the performance of Services; (b) designate a knowledgeable and empowered project manager and other personnel to cooperate with Licensor; (c) promptly review and approve Deliverables within the periods set forth herein; (d) ensure that its systems meet the technical requirements specified in the applicable Statement of Work; and (e) provide a suitable work environment

for any on-site Licensor personnel. Licensor's failure to meet any timeline or milestone shall be excused to the extent attributable to Licensee's failure to fulfill its obligations under this Section.

Licensee shall pay to Licensor the License Fees set forth in Schedule B. Unless otherwise specified, License Fees for each year of the Term shall be invoiced annually in advance on the first business day of each contract year, commencing on the Effective Date. All License Fees are non-refundable except as expressly provided in this Agreement.

Licensor shall invoice Licensee for Professional Services Fees in accordance with the payment schedule set forth in the applicable Statement of Work. Licensor shall provide itemized invoices reflecting the services performed, personnel hours, and any pre-approved expenses. Licensee shall pay each invoice within thirty (30) days of the invoice date.

Licensor shall be entitled to reimbursement of pre-approved out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with the performance of Professional Services, including reasonable travel, lodging, and subsistence expenses. All expenses exceeding five hundred dollars ($500) individually or two thousand dollars ($2,000) in aggregate per month shall require Licensee's prior written approval. Licensor shall provide supporting receipts for all reimbursable expenses.

Any amounts not paid within thirty (30) days of the due date shall accrue interest at the rate of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, whichever is less, calculated from the due date to the date of actual payment. Licensor reserves the right to suspend access to the Licensed Software and Hosted Services upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice if any undisputed payment is overdue by more than sixty (60) days.

All Fees are exclusive of applicable sales, use, value-added, goods and services, excise, and other taxes and duties imposed by any governmental authority. Licensee shall be responsible for and shall pay all such taxes, except for taxes based on Licensor's net income. If Licensee is required by law to withhold any taxes from payments to Licensor, Licensee shall: (a) provide Licensor with advance written notice; (b) remit such taxes to the appropriate governmental authority; and (c) provide Licensor with official tax receipts or other documentation.

Licensor may adjust the License Fees and Maintenance Fees for any renewal term by providing written notice to Licensee at least ninety (90) days prior to the commencement of such renewal term. Such adjustment shall not exceed the lesser of: (a) five percent (5%) above the fees for the then-current term; or (b) the percentage increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI-U) as published by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics for the preceding twelve-month period.

Licensee may, in good faith, dispute any invoice by providing written notice to Licensor within fifteen (15) business days of receipt, specifying the disputed amount and the grounds for the dispute. Licensee shall pay the undisputed portion of any invoice by the applicable due date. The Parties shall work together in good faith to resolve any billing disputes within thirty (30) days of Licensor's receipt of the dispute notice. Disputed amounts that are ultimately resolved in favor of Licensor shall be paid promptly along with accrued interest.

As between the Parties, Licensor shall retain all right, title, and interest in and to: (a) the Licensed Software, including all source code, object code, APIs, and algorithms; (b) all Documentation and training materials; (c) the

Platform and all components thereof; (d) all Updates and Upgrades; (e) all Licensor Background IP; and (f) any improvements, modifications, or derivative works of the foregoing, whether created by Licensor alone or jointly with Licensee. Licensee hereby irrevocably assigns and agrees to assign to Licensor all right, title, and interest in any improvements or modifications to the Licensed Software that Licensee or its Authorized Users may create, together with all Intellectual Property rights therein.

Licensee shall retain all right, title, and interest in and to: (a) Licensee's data, including all data inputted into the Licensed Software by Licensee or its Authorized Users; (b) Licensee's Background IP; and (c) any business methods, workflows, or processes of Licensee that do not incorporate Licensor's Confidential Information or Intellectual Property.

Ownership of Deliverables produced under any Statement of Work shall be as specified in such Statement of Work. In the absence of a specific provision, Deliverables that constitute custom software development specifically for Licensee and paid for in full by Licensee shall be owned by Licensee, provided that such Deliverables shall be subject to a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free license in favor of Licensor to the extent they incorporate or are derived from Licensor Background IP or the Licensed Software.

Licensee hereby grants to Licensor a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, fully paid-up, worldwide license to use, incorporate, and exploit any suggestions, enhancement requests, recommendations, feedback, or other input provided by Licensee or its Authorized Users relating to the Licensed Software or Licensor's products or services, without any obligation of attribution or compensation to Licensee.

Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any license or right, by implication, estoppel, or otherwise, under any Intellectual Property right of either Party except as expressly set forth herein. Each Party reserves all rights not expressly granted hereunder.

Each Party ("Receiving Party") shall hold in strict confidence all Confidential Information disclosed by the other Party ("Disclosing Party") and shall not disclose such Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall use Confidential Information solely for the purposes of fulfilling its obligations or exercising its rights under this Agreement.

Each Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care. Each Party shall limit access to Confidential Information to those of its employees, contractors, and advisors who have a genuine need to know such information and who are bound by confidentiality obligations no less restrictive than those set forth herein.

The obligations of this Article VI shall not apply to information that: (a) is or becomes publicly known through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (b) is rightfully received by the Receiving Party from a third party without restriction on disclosure; (c) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information; (d) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure, as evidenced by written records predating such disclosure; or (e) is expressly authorized for release by the Disclosing Party in writing.

If the Receiving Party is compelled by law, regulation, court order, or other governmental authority to disclose Confidential Information, the Receiving Party shall: (a) promptly notify the Disclosing Party in writing, to the extent permitted by law, prior to such disclosure; (b) cooperate with the Disclosing Party in seeking a protective order or other appropriate relief; and (c) disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed and use reasonable efforts to ensure that such disclosed information is treated confidentially.

Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and certify the destruction of) all tangible materials containing Confidential Information, except to the extent that applicable law requires retention of certain records.

The Parties acknowledge that any breach of the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Article VI would result in irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party for which monetary damages would be inadequate. The Disclosing Party shall therefore be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity, without the requirement of posting any bond or proving actual damages.

The confidentiality obligations set forth in this Article VI shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years, except with respect to trade secrets, which shall be maintained in confidence for so long as such information constitutes a trade secret under applicable law.

Each Party represents and warrants to the other Party that: (a) it is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of organization; (b) it has full power and authority to execute, deliver, and perform its obligations under this Agreement; (c) this Agreement has been duly authorized by all necessary corporate action and constitutes a legally binding obligation enforceable against it in accordance with its terms; (d) its entry into and performance of this Agreement does not violate any applicable law or any agreement to which it is a party; and (e) it is not subject to any judgment, order, or decree that would interfere with its ability to perform its obligations hereunder.

Licensor additionally represents and warrants that: (a) Licensor is the owner or authorized licensee of all Intellectual Property in the Licensed Software and has the right to grant the licenses set forth herein; (b) to the best of Licensor's knowledge, the Licensed Software does not infringe, misappropriate, or violate any third-party Intellectual Property rights; (c) the Licensed Software, when used in accordance with the Documentation, will perform in all material respects in accordance with the Documentation; (d) the Licensed Software does not contain any undisclosed malicious code, viruses, worms, Trojan horses, backdoors, or similar harmful components; (e) Licensor shall perform the Professional Services in a professional and workmanlike manner, consistent with industry standards, using qualified personnel; and (f) Licensor shall comply with all applicable laws and regulations in the performance of its obligations hereunder.

EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH IN THIS AGREEMENT, THE LICENSED SOFTWARE, DOCUMENTATION, PROFESSIONAL SERVICES, AND ALL OTHER MATERIALS AND SERVICES PROVIDED BY LICENSOR ARE PROVIDED 'AS IS,' WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND. LICENSOR EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE, NON-INFRINGEMENT, AND ANY WARRANTIES ARISING FROM COURSE OF DEALING, USAGE, OR TRADE PRACTICE. LICENSOR DOES NOT WARRANT THAT THE LICENSED SOFTWARE WILL BE ERROR-FREE OR UNINTERRUPTED, OR THAT ALL DEFECTS WILL BE CORRECTED.

Licensor shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Licensee and its officers, directors, employees, agents, successors, and permitted assigns (collectively, "Licensee Indemnitees") from and against any and all third-party claims, actions, suits, proceedings, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) (collectively, "Losses") arising out of or related to: (a) any allegation that the Licensed Software, as provided by Licensor and used in accordance with this Agreement, infringes or misappropriates any Intellectual Property right of a third party; (b) Licensor's material breach of any representation, warranty, or obligation under this Agreement; (c) the gross negligence or willful misconduct of Licensor or its personnel; or (d) Licensor's violation of applicable law.

Licensee shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Licensor and its officers, directors, employees, agents, successors, and permitted assigns (collectively, "Licensor Indemnitees") from and against any and all Losses arising out of or related to: (a) Licensee's use of the Licensed Software in a manner not authorized by this Agreement; (b) Licensee's combination of the Licensed Software with any products, data, hardware, software, or services not provided by Licensor, if such combination gives rise to the infringement claim; (c) Licensee's material breach of any representation, warranty, or obligation under this Agreement; (d) the gross negligence or willful misconduct of Licensee or its personnel; or (e) Licensee's violation of applicable law.

If the Licensed Software becomes, or in Licensor's reasonable opinion is likely to become, the subject of an intellectual property infringement claim, Licensor may, at its option and expense: (a) procure for Licensee the right to continue using the Licensed Software; (b) modify the Licensed Software so that it becomes non-infringing without materially diminishing its functionality; or (c) if neither (a) nor (b) is commercially practicable, terminate the applicable license and refund to Licensee a pro-rated portion of the License Fees paid for the period following such termination. The remedies set forth in this Section 8.3 constitute Licensee's sole and exclusive remedy for any claim of intellectual property infringement.

The Party seeking indemnification ("Indemnified Party") shall: (a) promptly notify the other Party ("Indemnifying Party") in writing of any claim for which indemnification is sought; (b) grant the Indemnifying Party sole control over the defense and settlement of such claim; and (c) provide reasonable cooperation to the Indemnifying Party at the Indemnifying Party's expense. The Indemnified Party shall have the right to participate in the defense of any claim with counsel of its own choosing at its own expense. The Indemnifying Party shall not settle any claim in a manner that imposes any obligation or liability on the Indemnified Party without the Indemnified Party's prior written consent.

IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOSS OF PROFITS, LOSS OF REVENUE, LOSS OF DATA, LOSS OF BUSINESS, OR COST OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES, AND REGARDLESS OF WHETHER SUCH DAMAGES ARISE UNDER CONTRACT, TORT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR ANY OTHER THEORY.

EXCEPT AS PROVIDED IN SECTION 9.3, EACH PARTY'S TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY TO THE OTHER PARTY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF: (A) THE TOTAL FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY LICENSEE IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE LIABILITY; OR (B) FIVE HUNDRED THOUSAND DOLLARS ($500,000).

The limitations of liability set forth in Sections 9.1 and 9.2 shall not apply to: (a) a Party's indemnification obligations under Article VIII with respect to third-party intellectual property infringement claims; (b) a Party's breach of its confidentiality obligations under Article VI; (c) damages arising from willful misconduct or fraud by a Party; (d) Licensee's failure to pay amounts due under this Agreement; or (e) any liability that cannot be limited by applicable law.

Each Party acknowledges that the limitations of liability and exclusions of damages set forth in this Article IX are a fundamental basis of the bargain between the Parties and that, absent such limitations, the economic terms of this Agreement would be substantially different.
ARTICLE X — DATA PRIVACY AND SECURITY

Each Party shall comply with all applicable data protection and privacy laws and regulations in connection with its activities under this Agreement, including, without limitation, the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), the California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA), the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) to the extent applicable, and any other applicable federal, state, or foreign data protection laws.

To the extent that Licensor processes Personal Data on behalf of Licensee in connection with the Licensed Software or Professional Services, the Parties shall be bound by the Data Processing Addendum set forth in Exhibit D, which is hereby incorporated by reference. In the event of any conflict between the terms of the Data Processing Addendum and this Agreement with respect to the processing of Personal Data, the Data Processing Addendum shall control.

Licensor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational security measures designed to protect the security, confidentiality, and integrity of Licensee's data processed in connection with the Licensed Software. At a minimum, such measures shall include: (a) encryption of data in transit using industry-standard protocols; (b) encryption of data at rest; (c) access controls and authentication mechanisms; (d) regular security assessments and penetration testing; (e) incident response procedures; and (f) employee security training. Licensor shall provide Licensee with a written description of its security measures upon request.

Licensor shall notify Licensee promptly, and in any event within forty-eight (48) hours, after becoming aware of any actual or reasonably suspected unauthorized access to, or acquisition, disclosure, or use of, Licensee's data (a "Security Incident"). Such notification shall include, to the extent then known: (a) a description of the Security Incident; (b) the categories and approximate number of individuals affected; (c) the likely consequences of the Security Incident; and (d) the measures taken or proposed to address the Security Incident. Licensor shall cooperate with Licensee in investigating and remediating any Security Incident.

Licensor shall retain Licensee's data only for as long as necessary to provide the Licensed Software and Professional Services, or as otherwise required by applicable law. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, Licensor shall, at Licensee's election, return or securely delete all of Licensee's data within thirty (30) days, and provide written certification of such deletion upon request.

During the Term, and subject to payment of the Maintenance Fees set forth in Schedule B, Licensor shall provide Licensee with the following maintenance services: (a) all Updates to the Licensed Software; (b) telephone and email-based technical support during normal business hours (8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Pacific Time, Monday through Friday, excluding Licensor's designated holidays); (c) a dedicated online support portal providing access to documentation, knowledge base articles, and ticket submission; and (d) reasonable assistance with the installation and configuration of Updates.

Licensor shall provide support services in accordance with the following priority tiers: (a) Priority 1 (Critical): System down or critical functionality unavailable; Licensor to acknowledge within one (1) hour and provide a workaround or fix within four (4) hours; (b) Priority 2 (High): Major functionality impaired; Licensor to acknowledge within two (2) hours and provide a workaround within eight (8) business hours; (c) Priority 3 (Medium): Non-critical functionality affected; Licensor to acknowledge within four (4) business hours and provide a resolution within five (5) business days; (d) Priority 4 (Low): Minor issues or questions; Licensor to acknowledge within one (1) business day and provide a resolution within ten (10) business days.

Licensor's maintenance and support obligations shall not extend to: (a) issues caused by Licensee's modification of the Licensed Software without Licensor's consent; (b) issues caused by Licensee's combination of the Licensed Software with unauthorized third-party products or services; (c) issues caused by Licensee's failure to implement Updates; (d) issues arising from misuse, abuse, or improper operation of the Licensed Software; (e) issues relating to Licensee's hardware or network infrastructure; or (f) custom code or integrations developed by Licensee or third parties other than Licensor.

Licensor shall offer Upgrades to the Licensed Software from time to time. Licensee's right to receive and install Upgrades shall be as specified in Schedule B. Where Upgrades are not included in Licensee's maintenance subscription, Licensor shall offer Licensee the right to purchase Upgrades at pricing to be mutually agreed in writing.

Subject to the terms and conditions of this Article XII and payment of the Technology Transfer Fee specified in Schedule B, Licensor may agree to transfer certain technology, documentation, or know-how to Licensee as set forth in a Technology Transfer Addendum executed by both Parties. Any technology transfer shall be limited to the scope expressly described in such addendum and shall not confer any broader rights than those expressly granted.

Licensor shall, within thirty (30) days of the Effective Date, deposit the source code for the Licensed Software with an independent escrow agent mutually agreed upon by the Parties. Licensor shall update the escrow deposit with each new major release of the Licensed Software. The escrow agreement shall provide for release of the source code to Licensee upon the occurrence of any of the following: (a) Licensor's commencement of bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, receivership, or assignment for the benefit of creditors; (b) Licensor's permanent cessation of business; or (c) Licensor's material breach of its maintenance obligations that remains uncured for sixty (60) days following notice. Use of released source code shall be limited to Licensee's internal use for maintenance and support of the Licensed Software.

As part of the Professional Services, Licensor shall conduct structured knowledge transfer sessions to enable Licensee's technical personnel to: (a) operate and administer the Licensed Software; (b) perform routine configuration and customization tasks; (c) conduct first-level troubleshooting; and (d) understand the architecture and integration patterns of the Licensed Software. Knowledge transfer sessions shall be scheduled in the applicable Statement of Work.

Any technology, documentation, or know-how transferred to Licensee pursuant to this Article XII shall be deemed Confidential Information of Licensor and shall be used by Licensee solely for its internal business purposes in connection with the Licensed Software. Licensee shall not sublicense, transfer, or disclose such technology to any third party without Licensor's prior written consent.

Licensor shall have the right, upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice and no more than once per calendar year (unless a prior audit revealed a material underpayment or non-compliance), to audit Licensee's use of the Licensed Software to verify compliance with the terms of this Agreement. Such audit may be conducted by Licensor's employees or by a mutually agreed-upon independent third-party auditor, subject to reasonable confidentiality obligations.

Licensee shall reasonably cooperate with any audit conducted pursuant to this Article, including by providing access to relevant records, systems, and personnel during normal business hours. Licensor and any auditor shall take reasonable steps to minimize disruption to Licensee's operations during the audit.

If an audit reveals that Licensee has underpaid Fees or exceeded its authorized usage of the Licensed Software: (a) Licensee shall pay the deficiency within thirty (30) days; and (b) if the underpayment exceeds five percent (5%) of amounts that should have been paid, Licensee shall reimburse Licensor for the reasonable costs of the audit. The Parties shall negotiate in good faith to address any audit findings, and any disputes regarding audit results shall be resolved pursuant to Article XVII.

This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for the Initial Term of three (3) years. Thereafter, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one-year periods (each, a "Renewal Term") unless either Party provides written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term. The Initial Term and all Renewal Terms shall be collectively referred to as the "Term."

Either Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the other Party if: (a) the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after receiving written notice specifically identifying the breach; provided that, for breaches that cannot reasonably be cured within thirty (30) days, the breaching Party shall have an additional sixty (60) days to cure, provided it commences cure within the initial thirty (30) day period and diligently pursues completion; (b) the other Party becomes insolvent, makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or becomes subject to bankruptcy, receivership, or similar proceedings that are not dismissed within sixty (60) days; or (c) the other Party ceases to carry on its business in the ordinary course.

Licensee may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days' written notice to Licensor. In the event of termination for convenience by Licensee, Licensee shall pay all fees accrued through the date of termination plus any early termination fees specified in Schedule B. For the avoidance of doubt, License Fees are non-refundable, and Licensee shall remain obligated to pay License Fees for the remainder of the then-current contract year.

Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement: (a) all licenses granted to Licensee hereunder shall immediately terminate, and Licensee shall cease all use of the Licensed Software; (b) each Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information of the other Party; (c) all provisions of this Agreement that by their nature should survive termination shall survive, including Articles V, VI, VIII, IX, XVI, and XVII, and Sections 2.4, 4.5, 14.4, and 14.5; (d) Licensee shall pay all outstanding Fees within thirty (30) days; and (e) each Party shall promptly execute and deliver any instruments or documents necessary to effectuate the foregoing.

Upon Licensee's request and subject to agreement on fees, Licensor shall provide reasonable transition assistance to Licensee for a period of up to six (6) months following the effective date of termination, to facilitate the orderly migration of Licensee's data and operations to an alternative solution.

Each Party shall comply with all applicable federal, state, local, and foreign laws, regulations, orders, and governmental requirements in the performance of its obligations and exercise of its rights under this Agreement, including without limitation laws and regulations relating to data privacy, security, anti-corruption, export control, and employment.

Licensee acknowledges that the Licensed Software may be subject to export control laws and regulations, including the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) administered by the U.S. Department of Commerce and the sanctions programs administered by the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC). Licensee shall not export, re-export, transfer, or make available the Licensed Software, Documentation, or any related technology to any country, entity, or individual in violation of applicable export control laws and
shall obtain any required export licenses or other governmental approvals prior to doing so.

Each Party represents and warrants that it has not paid, offered, promised, or authorized, and shall not pay, offer, promise, or authorize, directly or indirectly, any payment, gift, or thing of value to any government official, political party, or candidate for political office for the purpose of improperly obtaining or retaining business, securing any improper advantage, or influencing any official act in connection with this Agreement, in violation of the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, the UK Bribery Act, or any other applicable anti-corruption law.

Both Parties shall comply with all applicable equal employment opportunity and non-discrimination laws and regulations. Licensor, as a vendor providing services, shall not discriminate against any employee, applicant, subcontractor, or other person
race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

During the Term and for a period of three (3) years thereafter, each Party shall maintain, at its own expense, the following insurance coverages with insurers having an A.M. Best rating of at least A- / VII: (a) Commercial General Liability insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and $5,000,000 in the aggregate; (b) Professional Liability (Errors and Omissions) insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per claim and $5,000,000 in the aggregate; (c) Cyber Liability and Data Breach insurance with limits of not less than $3,000,000 per occurrence and $5,000,000 in the aggregate; (d) Workers' Compensation insurance as required by applicable law; and (e) Employer's Liability insurance with limits of not less than $1,000,000 per occurrence.

Each Party shall provide the other Party with certificates of insurance evidencing the coverages required herein within ten (10) business days of request. The certificates shall name the other Party as an additional insured on general liability policies. Each Party shall notify the other Party at least thirty (30) days prior to any cancellation or material reduction in coverage.

In the event of any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the breach, termination, or validity thereof (a "Dispute"), the Parties shall first attempt to resolve the Dispute through good-faith negotiations. A Party asserting a Dispute shall provide the other Party with a detailed written description of the Dispute. The Parties' designated representatives shall meet, either in person or by videoconference, within fifteen (15) business days of such notice to attempt to resolve the Dispute.

If the Dispute is not resolved within thirty (30) days of the initial meeting, either Party may escalate the Dispute to senior executives of each Party, who shall meet within fifteen (15) business days and negotiate in good faith for an additional thirty (30) days to resolve the Dispute.

If the Dispute is not resolved through executive escalation, either Party may submit the Dispute to non-binding mediation administered by JAMS, Inc. pursuant to its applicable mediation rules. The mediation shall take place in San Francisco, California, unless the Parties agree otherwise. The costs of mediation shall be shared equally by the Parties.

If the Dispute is not resolved through mediation within sixty (60) days of the commencement of the mediation, either Party may submit the Dispute to binding arbitration administered by JAMS, Inc. pursuant to its Comprehensive Arbitration Rules and Procedures. The arbitration shall be conducted before a single arbitrator with expertise in technology law and contracts. The arbitration shall take place in San Francisco, California. The arbitrator's award shall be final, binding, and enforceable in any court of competent jurisdiction. The Parties expressly waive any right to a jury trial.

Notwithstanding the dispute resolution procedures set forth in this Article XVII, either Party may seek emergency injunctive or other provisional relief from a court of competent jurisdiction where necessary to prevent irreparable harm pending completion of the dispute resolution process.

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to any choice of law provisions that would result in the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction. The United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods shall not apply to this Agreement.

All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given when: (a) delivered personally; (b) sent by nationally recognized overnight courier, with written confirmation of receipt; (c) sent by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid; or (d) sent by email with receipt confirmed by the recipient. Notices shall be addressed to the Parties at the addresses set forth on the signature page, or such other address as a Party may specify by written notice.

Neither Party shall be liable for any delay or failure in performance resulting from a Force Majeure Event, provided that: (a) the affected Party promptly notifies the other Party in writing of the Force Majeure Event and its expected duration; (b) the affected Party uses commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate the effects of and overcome the Force Majeure Event; and (c) the Force Majeure Event was not foreseeable or preventable by the affected Party. If a Force Majeure Event continues for more than ninety (90) days, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice without further liability, except for amounts already due and owing.

This Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by authorized representatives of both Parties. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the Party granting such waiver. Any waiver shall be limited to the specific instance and shall not constitute a continuing waiver or a waiver of any other provision.

This Agreement, together with all Exhibits, Schedules, and Addenda attached hereto, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, negotiations, representations, and understandings, whether oral or written, relating to such subject matter. No prior drafts of this Agreement shall be used in the interpretation or construction of this Agreement.

Neither Party may assign this Agreement or any of its rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Party, which shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. Notwithstanding the foregoing: (a) either Party may assign this Agreement without consent in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets or business, provided that the

assignee assumes all obligations of the assigning Party hereunder; and (b) Licensor may subcontract the performance of Professional Services to qualified subcontractors, provided that Licensor remains responsible for the performance of all subcontracted services. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section shall be null and void.

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable under applicable law, such provision shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable, and the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions shall not in any way be affected or impaired thereby, provided that the economic substance of this Agreement is not materially impaired.

This Agreement is for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Parties and their respective permitted successors and assigns. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create any rights in, or to confer any benefits upon, any person or entity other than the Parties.

The Parties are independent contractors. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create a partnership, joint venture, agency, franchise, employment, or fiduciary relationship between the Parties. Neither Party shall have the authority to bind the other Party or to incur any obligation on its behalf.

Neither Party shall issue any press release or make any public statement regarding this Agreement or the relationship between the Parties without the prior written consent of the other Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Licensor may reference Licensee as a customer in its marketing materials, using only Licensee's name and general description of the services provided, subject to Licensee's prior written approval of each such reference.

This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Electronic signatures shall be deemed valid and binding to the same extent as original signatures.
The headings and section titles in this Agreement are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the meaning or interpretation of this Agreement.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Master Services and License Agreement as of the Effective Date first written above.
The following modules and components constitute the
Platform licensed to Licensee under this Agreement:
Real-time business intelligence, dashboards, and custom reporting engine
General ledger, accounts payable/receivable, financial consolidation
Customer relationship management, pipeline tracking, and communications
Inventory, procurement, supplier management, and logistics tracking
Human capital management, payroll processing, benefits administration
API gateway, data integration, ETL pipelines, and connector library
Identity management, SSO, RBAC, MFA, and audit logging
Native iOS and Android applications with offline capability
No-code workflow automation, approvals, and process orchestration
Document storage, versioning, eSignature integration, and archival
EXHIBIT B — FEES AND PAYMENT SCHEDULE
Additional User Licenses (per user, per year)
All fees are denominated in United States Dollars. Wire transfer instructions and remittance details shall be provided on each invoice. Checks should be made payable to

Licensor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that the Hosted Services are available 99.5% of the time in any given calendar month, measured as: Uptime % = ((Total Minutes - Downtime Minutes) / Total Minutes) x 100. "Downtime" means the total accumulated time during a calendar month during which the Hosted Services are unavailable, excluding scheduled maintenance windows, Force Majeure Events, and outages caused by Licensee's actions or third-party services outside Licensor's reasonable control.

Licensor shall be entitled to conduct scheduled maintenance of the Hosted Services during the following maintenance windows: (a) weekly maintenance window: Sundays from 2:00 a.m. to 6:00 a.m. Pacific Time; and (b) quarterly maintenance window: up to eight (8) hours of additional maintenance per quarter, with at least five (5) business days' prior written notice to Licensee. Licensor shall use reasonable efforts to minimize service disruption during all maintenance windows.

If the monthly uptime falls below the 99.5% commitment, Licensee shall be entitled to a service credit calculated as follows: (a) 99.0% to 99.4% availability: 10% credit on that month's Hosted Services Fee; (b) 98.0% to 98.9% availability: 20% credit; (c) 95.0% to 97.9% availability: 30% credit; (d) below 95.0% availability: 50% credit. Credits shall be applied to the next invoice. Credits are Licensee's sole remedy for SLA failures, except in cases of Licensor's willful misconduct or gross negligence.

Licensor shall maintain continuous monitoring of the Hosted Services and shall provide Licensee with access to a real-time status dashboard. Licensor shall provide monthly uptime reports within five (5) business days after the end of each calendar month.
This Data Processing Addendum ("DPA") forms part of the Agreement and applies to the extent that Licensor processes Personal Data on behalf of Licensee
providing the Licensed Software and Professional Services. This DPA is intended to ensure that both Parties comply with their respective obligations under applicable data protection laws.

For the purposes of this DPA: (a) Licensee is the "Controller" (or "Business" under CCPA) and is responsible for determining the purposes and means of processing Personal Data; and (b) Licensor is the "Processor" (or "Service Provider" under CCPA) and processes Personal Data solely on behalf of Licensee and in accordance with Licensee's documented instructions.
Licensor shall process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Licensee, including

transfers to third countries. If Licensor is required by applicable law to process Personal Data other than in accordance with Licensee's instructions, Licensor shall inform Licensee before such processing, unless prohibited by law.
Licensee grants Licensor general authorization to engage
to assist with the processing of Personal Data, subject to the following conditions: (a) Licensor shall maintain a list of current
and make it available to Licensee upon request; (b) Licensor shall notify Licensee at least thirty (30) days before adding or replacing a
shall be bound by data protection obligations no less restrictive than those set forth in this DPA; and (d) Licensor shall remain liable to Licensee for the performance of each

Licensor shall assist Licensee, by appropriate technical and organizational measures, in responding to requests from data subjects exercising their rights under applicable data protection law, including rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, data portability, and objection. Licensor shall promptly forward to Licensee any data subject requests it receives relating to Licensee's Personal Data.

Where the processing of Personal Data involves transfers to countries outside the European Economic Area or the United Kingdom that are not recognized as providing an adequate level of data protection, such transfers shall be subject to appropriate safeguards, including Standard Contractual Clauses as approved by the European Commission or equivalent mechanisms under applicable law. The Parties agree to execute any additional documentation required to effectuate such transfers.

The following open-source and third-party software components are incorporated into the Licensed Software. Licensee's use of these components is subject to the applicable license terms set forth below.

Complete license texts for all Third-Party Components are available in the /licenses directory of the Licensed Software installation package and are incorporated herein by reference. Licensor warrants that all Third-Party Components are used in compliance with their respective license terms.
Technologies Corporation — Meridian Global Partners, Inc.
Master Services and License Agreement | Agreement No.: NTC-MGP-2026-0047 | Confidential
